title: chaenorhinum minus subsp. idaeum
intro: Chaenorhinum minus subsp. idaeum, also known as small toadflax or small toadflax Chaenorhinum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, characterized by its small blue or purple flowers. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Chaenorhinum minus subsp. idaeum are thought to include relief from respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal remedy.
